description: The wider cargo test --lib compiler2:: sweep terminates with SIGSEGV after reporting test failures. First re-run after clearing known failures from the current callable-interface/source-production arc; if the segfault persists, isolate the crashing test or harness path and fix it. This ticket depends on the current sweep blockers because those panics may be poisoning later state.

comments.text: After the current batch, cargo test --lib compiler2:: -- --nocapture still ends in a signal crash after known failing tests. The latest reproduction terminated with SIGBUS, not SIGSEGV, after the remaining port_macros/port_misc/port_type_infer failures.

During fz-rh2.17.5.6.3 verification, plain parallel cargo test --lib compiler2:: crashed with SIGSEGV before producing the known failure summary. The controlled single-thread run cargo test --lib compiler2:: -- --test-threads=1 completed normally with the existing 10 failures, so the crash is tied to the parallel suite/harness path rather than this provider-boundary assertion path.

title: Compiler2 test sweep: investigate post-failure signal crash during wider compiler2:: runs
